title: Road to the Final
_teams: 32 knockout teams. Order matters: indexes 0-15 fill the Right half, 16-31 the Left half. Adjacent pairs (0,1),(2,3)… meet in the Round of 32.
_r32: The 16 Round-of-32 matches, keyed 'side:1:k' (side R/L, k=0..7). The two teams are teams[off+2k] vs teams[off+2k+1] (off=0 for R, 16 for L). Scheduled match: {date, time}. Played match: {score, winner, et?, pens?} — 'winner' is a team code and drives who advances/who's eliminated.

goals.desc: A fine block by Sano to deny Lucas Paqueta's strike, which came after a spell of Brazilian pressing and probing. Sano registered more interceptions than any other player in the 2025/26 Bundesliga.
Casemiro's point-blank header seems bound for the net, but it strikes Tomiyasu on the line and then, off the rebound, bounces kindly off goalkeeper Suzuki. Somehow it stays out!

Gabriel Martinelli puts the Seleção ahead! Rayan wins possession back in a dangerous area, and his pass lets Bruno Guimaraes slide the ball through to Martinelli, who breaks Japanese hearts with an unerring low right-footed finish that beats Suzuki via the keeper's fingers and the left-hand post.

A beautiful finish from Antonio Nusa. Norway have just enjoyed their best spell of the match and made it pay. The ball is worked out to Nusa at the edge of the box, and he bends a delightful effort into the top corner. It was in from the instant it left his boot — Nusa had already wheeled away to celebrate before it reached the net!

They'll meet Brazil in the Round of 16! As you'd expect, Erling Haaland is the match-winner, his 86th-minute strike sealing a Norway victory after they were pushed all the way by an excellent Côte d'Ivoire side. Antonio Nusa had opened the scoring with a superb curling effort before Amad Diallo levelled with an arguably even better goal, showing skill and poise to bring his side level. Haaland had the last word, though, finishing off a slick Norway move. What a game. What a tournament.

Haaland restores Norway's lead! It's a fine team move: Oscar Bobb threads a ball through to Patrick Berg, who had timed a superb run forward, and Berg squares it for Haaland, ideally placed to slot home. 2-1!

The co-hosts work through Ecuador's press to release Julian Quinones. There's plenty still to do with Willian Pacho shadowing his every step, but a quick drop of the shoulder creates the room to shoot inside the box, and he rockets an emphatic finish past the goalkeeper.

Raul Jimenez hands El Tri daylight. He seizes on an errant pass played out from the back, finds Julian Quinones and keeps running. The Mexico No9 surges into space at the edge of the area, collects the return ball and finishes with aplomb.

What a start for Congo DR, and what a way for Brian Cipenga to notch his first international goal! One touch with his left foot, then a sharp finish with his right inside Jordan Pickford's near post. The forward capitalises on a right-to-left switch of play, breaking into the box to sweep his shot home.
England are level again: Lionel Mpasi is finally beaten by a well-placed Harry Kane header, meeting substitute Anthony Gordon's cross from the left that followed a ball in from Declan Rice.

Harry Kane strikes again! The skipper fires into the roof of the net after collecting Anthony Gordon's back-heel and driving into the area. It all began with the earlier phase of play — Jude Bellingham's surge and shot. That's Kane's 13th World Cup finals goal and his fifth of this North American campaign.

More magic from the genius No10! That's Lionel Messi's seventh goal of World Cup 2026, and another special one — the technique needed to finish it, oh my. Lisandro Martinez lofts a pass over the top of the Cabo Verde defence; Messi, his run timed to perfection, kills the ball stone dead with a velvet touch before hammering it into the roof of the net. He is extraordinary.

Unbelievable! Deroy Duarte has just scored a goal that will send shockwaves across the World Cup — perhaps across the world! Cabo Verde have played with real intent and purpose since the second half began, and now they're level. Duarte crashes a shot into the bottom corner, sparking wild celebrations. This World Cup keeps serving up incredible moments.

Lisandro Martinez with the strike! Having set up Argentina's first goal, the centre-back now fires them back in front. A corner is deflected out to Martinez at the back post; he takes a superb touch to make the chance, then sends an unstoppable finish beyond Vozinha.

Cabo Verde are level again! And this is special from Sidny Lopes Cabral! They've refused to buckle since Lisandro Martinez's goal, and Lopes Cabral has equalised in spectacular style. The bend he gets on this strike is remarkable: wide on the left flank, he cuts inside the penalty box and curls a sumptuous effort into the top corner, leaving Dibu Martinez grasping at thin air. One of the goals of the tournament — and it's 2-2!

Ashour puts Egypt ahead. The Pharaohs recycle possession after Irvine blocks Ashour's initial effort from an angled free-kick. When Hafez sends the ball back in, Ashour heads it firmly past Beach for his second goal of these finals.

This one is all about Johan Manzambi, though! The 20-year-old sets off on a sensational run down the left channel, leaving Aissa Mandi trailing behind. He pulls the ball across goal and Breel Embolo taps it home. Against the run of play, but Switzerland lead.

Clinical from Ndoye! Switzerland have made a dream start to the second half. A fine finish from Dan Ndoye, who expertly controls a Rafik Belghali defensive clearance before slotting a precise shot into the bottom corner. The Swiss now hold a firm grip on this tie.
Colombia find the breakthrough. It's substitute Luis Suarez who dazzles down the right and delivers a teasing cross for Jhon Arias, who nets his first goal of the World Cup. What a start!

Senegal lead! Habib Diarra gets the goal, but plenty of credit belongs to Ismaila Sarr too. Sarr did brilliantly to climb high and steer a header goalward, only to see it strike the post. Diarra pounced on the rebound in a flash, lashing it in to make it 1-0 to the Lions of Teranga.

An emphatic finish from Ismaila Sarr! Wonderful play from the Senegalese forward. Moussa Niakhate lofts a raking pass over the Belgium backline, Sarr runs in behind, brings it down on his chest and then crashes a shot past Courtois. Brilliant.
Now then! Just as Belgium looked bound for the exit, Romelu Lukaku converts a Thomas Meunier cross to hand them hope!

What a comeback! Youri Tielemans has just drawn Belgium level. It's a hugely brave header from the captain, who fights off two Senegal defenders and goalkeeper Mory Diaw to steer his effort into the net. Senegal had looked in complete control, but now Belgium are buoyant!

Belgium have surely won it! A superb penalty from the captain, who buries his kick in the top corner. Senegal keeper Diaw dived the wrong way — though it likely wouldn't have mattered even if he'd guessed right. Precision placement from Tielemans. Ice in his veins!
USA score — and it's Balogun! Malik Tillman slides in a pass that takes a slight deflection, Balogun is on hand, stays composed and finishes to give USA a 1-0 lead.
Malik Tillman makes it 2-0 to USA!! A foul on Sergino Dest just outside the box brings a free-kick, and Tillman blasts it past the keeper into the net. USA, down to ten men, double their advantage!

Spain lead! It's Oyarzabal's third goal of these finals — and his 16th in his last 16 starts — as the forward finishes calmly, stroking Cucurella's smart pass from the left into the corner of the net.
Perro nets his first national-team goal! The right-back times his run to perfection to meet Baena's left-wing cross, worked out wide by Cucurella. A thumping header extends La Roja's lead.

Spain score again! Cucurella measures an inch-perfect delivery from the left to match Oyarzabal's run, and he strokes it nervelessly past Schlager for his second goal of the match and his fourth of the North American tournament.
Croatia's ironman brought the ball down and drilled it home at the far post to hand the 2018 finalists the lead in Toronto. Croatia have emerged for the second half determined to win it.
Having been flagged offside moments earlier, Ronaldo found the net from 11 yards to level for Portugal — his first goal in the knockout stage of the competition!
The substitute climbed above the Croatian defenders to head in what he'll hope proves the winner! My word, what a game this has been.

The Dutch make the breakthrough. Amid personal tragedy, Cody Gakpo has shown great courage to play tonight, and now he's the man to break the deadlock. It came from a wonderful run and an off-balance touch back across the face of goal by Crysencio Summerville, teeing up Gakpo, who swept it home and was immediately overcome with emotion.
Late drama as the Atlas Lions hit back. Centre-back Issa Diop pops up in an advanced position to head home substitute Chemsdine Talbi's cross at the start of added time.

Stephen Eustaquio is Canada's hero! A superb goal. A headed clearance dropped straight to Eustaquio at the edge of the box, but there was plenty still to do. He takes a brilliant touch, then rifles it into the bottom corner. Williams had no chance. Canada are euphoric!
Zetterstrom has no answer to this one! Dembele and Olise combine from the corner to release Mbappe. He dances past Gyokeres and drives into the bottom-right corner, leaving the Sweden stopper rooted!
France have gone closest to a third goal, Olise twice denied since Barcola's strike. Sweden have done well to stay in it, but must now turn the screw if they want a shot at the knockouts.
That's eighteen World Cup goals for Kylian Mbappe, from the same number of matches.

Entirely against the run of play, Paraguay grab the lead just before the interval as Julio Enciso heads past a helpless Manuel Neuer. Their gameplan has worked to perfection — soaking up Germany's pressure before striking on the counter-attack.
Germany are right back in this as Kai Havertz heads in a Florian Wirtz cross. Game on!

_schedule: Kickoff date/time for the inner rounds, keyed 'side:L:k' (L=2 R16, L=3 QF, L=4 SF). Add {score, winner} here the same way once these are played to light them up.

goals.desc: Norway's main man delivers again — and what a moment to do it! A brilliant finish from Erling Haaland, who climbs high to meet Andreas Schjelderup's cross and powers a downward header past Alisson. 1-0 to Norway!

What a player! Erling Haaland has surely fired Norway into the last eight! An exceptional finish: he collects the ball outside the box, takes a touch, then drives the purest strike imaginable into the bottom corner. It's 2-0, and Brazil's race looks run.
Mexico have been the better side so far, but it's England who lead against the run of play as Jude Bellingham heads home a clever Bukayo Saka cross. Bellingham has had a fantastic World Cup.
Just like that, England are two up — and it's Jude Bellingham again, finishing a neat one-two with Harry Kane. Two goals in as many minutes for the Real Madrid man.
Just as the game looked to be slipping away from Mexico, they're suddenly back in it — Julian Quinones fires the ball into the roof of the net from a free-kick. What a passage of play!
Harry Kane drives the resulting penalty into the corner past Rangel, extending his goalscoring record as the Three Lions edge towards victory.
Raul Jimenez — who has scored in every match he's faced Jordan Pickford — keeps up his 100-per-cent record, rifling into the corner past the England goalkeeper. It's 3-2!
Mikel Merino's late goal proved the difference between the two neighbours. Spain will now face the winner of USA v Belgium.
Morocco lead from a well-worked set piece: Hakimi rolls a free-kick across the edge of the area to Ounahi, who slots it home through a crowd of bodies.

Ounahi lashes into the roof of the net to make it 2-0, Morocco undoing Canada on the break. It's a devastating counter, finished off by Ounahi sweeping home after Diaz squares it to him from the right. Sheer quality.

Morocco put the icing on the cake on this anniversary day in the USA. With Canada committed forward, the Atlas Lions slice through on the counter, Diaz releasing substitute Rahimi to slot the third past Crepeau.

Kylian Mbappe tucks a penalty into the corner to put France ahead — it had been coming for Les Bleus. Mbappe has now netted ten goals in his last six FIFA World Cup matches, and 19 in 19 tournament appearances, leaving him just one behind Argentina legend Lionel Messi.

_groups: Final group-stage standings (finishing order 1-4) and matches, keyed A-L. Table row: {code,name,iso,w,d,l,gf,ga}; played=w+d+l, pts=3w+d, gd=gf-ga are derived. Match: {d,a,b,s} with score s in a-b order. Qualification (winner/runner-up/best third/eliminated) is derived from position + bracket membership.

goals.desc: Pandemonium! Mexico are in front! On the front foot from the start, they see Julian Quinones send Mexico City Stadium wild. South Africa tried to play out from the back, but excellent pressing from Erik Lira let Quinones steal possession and lash the ball through Ronwen Williams' legs.

There was a hint of tension at 1-0, but now Mexico are in dreamland! A wonderfully-crafted goal: Roberto Alvarado whips in a pinpoint cross that Jimenez converts with a trademark header. The emotion was written all over the striker's face as he celebrated.
Somewhat against the run of play, but Czechia strike first. A long throw-in is met by the head of the onrushing captain, who powers it past Kim Seunggyu.

Korea's silky midfield schemer Hwang Inbeom gathers a defence-splitting pass from Lee Kangin, takes one touch to control and another to bamboozle Kovar before dinking a chipped finish into an empty net. His first goal for his country since 2023 could prove decisive.
Having grabbed the opener, Hwang Inbeom makes the second, cutting the ball back from the byline for substitute Oh Hyeongyu to sweep home and edge Korea in front.

A superb team goal from Czechia. Adam Hlozek cuts it back from the right flank to Alexandr Sojka, whose forward touch opens a shooting chance for Michal Sadilek. The Sparta Praha midfielder takes his second national-team goal with a smart finish past Ronwen Williams.
Teboho Mokoena has just produced a vital defensive interception with Czechia building a head of steam.
Delirium in Guadalajara! Luis Romo pounced on the confusion in the box to slam the ball into an open net.
Mateo Chavez is the man to break the deadlock! The No20 races from inside his own half, hurdles a Czechia challenge, squares up Matej Kovar and coolly slots home to send Mexico City Stadium wild.

If the first half lacked spark, the second was the complete opposite. Mateo Chavez set it in motion, latching on to a Raul Jimenez pass, darting past a Czechia challenge and slotting home to send Mexico City Stadium into raptures. Six minutes later it was two: Jorge Sanchez was sent clear and, though denied by Matej Kovar, a poor Czechia clearance dropped to Julian Quinones, who had the simplest of tasks to double the co-hosts' lead. The party stepped up a gear when legendary goalkeeper Guillermo Ochoa, at his sixth World Cup, came on for his 153rd Mexico cap with 12 minutes remaining. Then, in stoppage time, Alvaro Fidalgo added the icing: Santiago Gimenez charged goalward but couldn't beat Kovar, yet Fidalgo was on hand to rifle into the top-left corner and cap one of El Tri's greatest nights.

If the first half lacked spark, the second was the complete opposite. Mateo Chavez set it in motion, latching on to a Raul Jimenez pass, darting past a Czechia challenge and slotting home to send Mexico City Stadium into raptures. Six minutes later it was two: Jorge Sanchez was sent clear and, though denied by Matej Kovar, a poor Czechia clearance dropped to Julian Quinones, who had the simplest of tasks to double the co-hosts' lead. The party stepped up a gear when legendary goalkeeper Guillermo Ochoa, at his sixth World Cup, came on for his 153rd Mexico cap with 12 minutes remaining. Then, in stoppage time, Alvaro Fidalgo added the icing: Santiago Gimenez charged goalward but couldn't beat Kovar, yet Fidalgo was on hand to rifle into the top-left corner and cap one of El Tri's greatest nights.
How big could that prove? Thapelo Maseko finally makes one count with his fifth attempt of the match, firing through the legs of Jens Castrop and out of Kim Seunggyu's reach.

Like buses — you wait an age, then they all arrive at once: first substitute Karim Boudiaf fires over, then Juan Vargas is denied one-on-one by Mahmoud Abunada before Breel Embolo shoots into the side-netting.

At just 20, Johan Manzambi has left his mark at the World Cup after a stellar season for Bundesliga side Freiburg, whom he helped reach the UEFA Europa League final. Named the Europa League Young Player of the Season, he's a clear star of the future. Manzambi (20 years and 247 days) is the youngest Swiss player to score at the World Cup since Kiki Antenen (20 years and 241 days) did so against Mexico in 1950, and the third-youngest Swiss scorer overall.
Switzerland double their lead with only minutes left! They're heading for full points as Ruben Vargas, off the bench, slots home a right-footed finish.
Manzambi again! This time Vargas turns playmaker with a sharp cutback, and Manzambi finishes coolly. What a closing 15 minutes from the Swiss!
It's raining goals now at Los Angeles Stadium! Bosnia and Herzegovina pull one back in second-half stoppage time through a thunderbolt volley from Ermin Mahmic.
Granit Xhaka converts from the spot, leaving no doubt as he tucks the penalty into the corner. Switzerland cap a wild finale in style!

BC Place erupts! Canada have begun well and get their reward! Cyle Larin nets his second goal of the tournament, prodding in from close range to send the home fans wild. David had lashed in a shot that Abunada did well to parry, but Larin was onto the rebound in a flash.
Jonathan David makes it 2-0 — and in style! A stunning volley from the Canada striker, who watches the ball all the way onto his boot before rifling in an effort with too much power for Abunada.

Canada are rampant! Jonathan David grabs his second and Canada's third. Cyle Larin rises high to power a header goalward; Abunada makes a tremendous save, but David is there to convert from close range. 3-0!

Jonathan David makes it 6-0! A hat-trick for the lethal Canada striker. Saliba let fly from range but David, sensing it wouldn't drop in, controlled with a fabulous touch before finishing with power and precision.

Jonathan David makes it 6-0! A hat-trick for the lethal Canada striker. Saliba let fly from range but David, sensing it wouldn't drop in, controlled with a fabulous touch before finishing with power and precision.

Only 40 seconds into the second half, Ruben Vargas nets his second of the finals — a brilliant low drive that beats Maxime Crepeau at his right-hand post after the forward meets Johan Manzambi's delivery from the right.

Breel Embolo sets up Johan Manzambi for his third goal of the finals, a low strike across Maxime Crepeau that the keeper can't stop. That's Manzambi's third of the finals and his sixth in 15 games for the Nati.

"Let's go Canada" rings out, and the Vancouver crowd haven't given up on this one — certainly not after that brilliant stretch and finish from Promise David. A quickfire equaliser nearly followed, too, only for Derek Cornelius to flash an inviting header wide under pressure from Johan Manzambi.
McGinn puts the Scots in front with a finish that took a deflection on its way in. A magical touch from Che Adams set it up, and after an initial block, McGinn was on hand to steer it home.

What a start by Morocco. They knock it around before Brahim Diaz clips a pass over the Scotland defence for Ismael Saibari, who thrashes an unstoppable finish into the roof of the net. No chance whatsoever for Angus Gunn.

The threat had been mounting, and it's Matheus Cunha who puts Brazil ahead. Vinicius Junior dribbles into the box and cuts it back centrally, where Hannes Delcroix diverts it onto the Brazilian forward, whose effort rolls past Johny Placide in the Haiti goal.

They haven't dazzled throughout, but they've done more than enough. Two incisive Vinicius Junior runs that fashioned Matheus Cunha's brace — his second and third goals for his country — kept Brazil in control. Vini Jr. then switched from provider to finisher, grabbing a stoppage-time third.

Brazil stretch their lead on the stroke of half-time. Lucas Paqueta punishes Haiti's high line, flicking the ball to an onrushing Vinicius Junior. The Real Madrid forward takes two touches and slots through the legs of Placide.

A wonderful team goal from Brazil. They move the ball with speed, precision and poise until it drops to Bruno Guimaraes, who picks out Cunha with a sensational reverse pass. The Manchester United man confidently slots home his third of the tournament.

What a terrific match in Atlanta. Haiti raced into a 1-0 lead with their first World Cup goal since 1974, only for Morocco to equalise. Isidor then struck a golazo to remember for the Caribbean side, but once more Morocco responded. The second half tilted towards the Africans, and goals from Rahimi and Yassine sealed the points. Morocco march into the Round of 32 in fine form, while Haiti head home with plenty of admirers.
How's that for a response???? Morocco break and Saibari finishes with a sweet stroke into the net. It's 2-2!!
Morocco lead 4-2 now!! A ball near the byline stays in, and Rahimi's cross is simple for Yassine to slot home.
Morocco lead 4-2 now!! A ball near the byline stays in, and Rahimi's cross is simple for Yassine to slot home.

This one WILL count! A fantastic run from Pulisic down the left, and the ball across picks out the charging Balogun, whose clinical finish doubles the USA lead! They're in dreamland. History repeats itself for Balogun and the USA — the frontman scored what proved the winner against Paraguay in a November friendly.

It's a party at Los Angeles Stadium as the USA grab a third just before half-time. Balogun stays central, takes a pass, keeps his composure and lashes a left-footed laser to make it 3-0! A familiar scoreline for these teams — USA beat Paraguay 3-0 at the 1930 World Cup.
It's 3-1... Paraguay are within two, the goal coming from a long ball forward, a scramble, and the ball dropping to Enciso, who sets up a perfect finish for Mauricio.
Gio Reyna seals the USA win with a sparkling outside-of-the-boot finish to cap a 20-pass move, sparking wild celebrations at Los Angeles Stadium. What a finish!

Starlet Nestory Irankunda shows exactly why he's so highly rated, firing Australia ahead midway through the first half. Paul Okon-Engstler arced a ball over the top for Irankunda to chase, and the live-wire forward ran onto it before cutting inside Merih Demiral and slotting clinically into the bottom corner.

The Socceroos have three points within reach after Connor Metcalfe's long-range drive. The ball broke to Metcalfe midway inside the Türkiye half, and he drove forward before unleashing a fierce strike from 25 yards that skidded off the turf and past Cakir into the net.

For only the second time in their history, USA have opened a World Cup with two straight wins, and the reward is qualification from Group D. For Australia, the damage came from two first-half goals — Cameron Burgess' own goal from Folarin Balogun's low cross, then Alex Freeman's close-range header from Sergino Dest's deflected strike — capping the co-hosts' fast start in Seattle. The Socceroos rallied after the break but couldn't find a goal of their own.
Paraguay lead after just 64 seconds, Matias Galarza firing in from distance. They're off to the races — the fastest goal of this World Cup so far!
Auston Trusty with the goal! The place has erupted! Berhalter's corner travels all the way to Trusty at the back post, and he takes a touch before leathering it into the net.

Türkiye have their first goal of the tournament! And it's superbly taken by Arda Guler. Yilmaz is the creator, firing an incisive pass into the Real Madrid man's feet; Guler controls and slots a clinical finish past Turner.

A bolt from Berhalter! Such a sweet strike. USA flew out of the blocks in the first half and have done exactly the same in the second. A long McKenzie throw is cleared out to Berhalter at the edge of the box; the ball sits up nicely, but there's still plenty to do — he gets over it and hammers it past Cakir in the Türkiye goal. The keeper had no chance.
Drama! Scrappy, but Türkiye won't mind! Ayhan finishes from close range after pinball in the penalty box. 3-2!

A beautiful goal from Felix Nmecha! Slick and deadly from Julian Nagelsmann's side. Nmecha exchanges a stunning one-two with Florian Wirtz before caressing a curling effort into the bottom corner. Perfection.

Time to catch a breath! A quite remarkable first half in Houston. Germany looked set to run riot after Felix Nmecha's early goal, but Curaçao showed incredible spirit to stay in it and level through Livano Comenencia. Germany soon reasserted control, though, and a Nico Schlotterbeck header plus a Kai Havertz penalty leave them 3-1 up at the break.
Schlotterbeck with the header! That will settle some German nerves. A fine header from Nico Schlotterbeck, who meets Nathaniel Brown's delivery and steers it past Eloy Room.
Cool as you like! The Arsenal man slots home from the spot, sending Eloy Room the wrong way. The penalty came for a Riechedly Bazoer foul on Felix Nmecha. Germany in complete control.

A precision finish from the Bayern Munich man! Musiala couldn't have placed it any better. Joshua Kimmich slid a pass into his path and he struck first-time, sending it across goal, past Eloy Room and into the bottom corner. Unbelievable quality.
Another lovely finish — and what an assist! Deniz Undav's impudent flick releases Nathaniel Brown, who volleys past Eloy Room. The quality of today's goals has been superb.
Germany aren't easing off! Deniz Undav makes it 6-1, converting after Joshua Kimmich laid it on a plate. Leandro Bacuna tried to block the strike, but it was too powerful and deflected in.
Simply sublime. Kai Havertz has taken that brilliantly. He seizes on a Deniz Undav pass, holds off Gervane Kastaneer's challenge and dinks a finish over Eloy Room.

That first half has flown by, the action relentless. Germany trail, but they haven't played poorly — much of their football has been slick, inventive and purposeful. Côte d'Ivoire, though, boast electrifying forwards, with Yan Diomande the pick so far. His dazzling run helped fashion the Franck Kessie goal that separates the sides. Curbing Diomande's threat may well be a key theme of Julian Nagelsmann's half-time talk. Time to catch a breath!
The substitute strikes! A lovely volleyed finish from Deniz Undav, converting an excellent cross from fellow sub Nadiem Amiri. 1-1.

The substitute has done it again! Superb from Deniz Undav, who looks to have won it for Germany — and booked their knockout place. Another brilliant finish: he takes a Nmecha pass on the turn, then swivels and shoots in one motion, leaving Fofana no chance in the Côte d'Ivoire goal. What an impact!

Côte d'Ivoire with the opener! The electric Yan Diomande pounces on sloppy Curaçao passing in their defensive third. The winger drives forward and lays it on a plate for Nicolas Pepe, who taps home from close range.

A wonderful finish from Nicolas Pepe. It's 2-0! Elite-level work from the Côte d'Ivoire forward, who takes a perfect touch off Sangare's driven pass before bending an unstoppable effort into the top corner.
Leroy Sane sidefoots home inside two minutes, after Aleksandar Pavlovic wins the ball outside the box and Florian Wirtz sets up Sane for a smart low finish.
Nilson Angulo levels with a great strike from outside the box, curling it through the legs of Aleksandar Pavlovic and beyond the despairing dive of Manuel Neuer.
Ecuador 2-1: The stadium goes wild as Ecuador score from a corner, flicked on by Kevin Rodriguez for Gonzalo Plata to turn in from close range right in front of Manuel Neuer.
Virgil van Dijk makes the breakthrough for the Netherlands: a recycled Ryan Gravenberch free-kick is whipped in from the right, and the unmarked centre-back guides his header past Zion Suzuki.

Takefusa Kubo glides into the Dutch box and rolls it back for Keito Nakamura, who takes two touches and fires past Bart Verbruggen to draw Japan level. A quiet first half has given way to a far livelier second, and it's well and truly game on.
A second assist for Ryan Gravenberch, laying it off to Summerville, who bends a fine left-footed effort from the top of the box to restore the Netherlands' lead.

A deflected Daichi Kamada strike two minutes from time earns Japan a share of the spoils in Dallas. Goalless at the break, the game burst to life in the second half, with all four goals coming in the final 45. Virgil van Dijk and then Crysencio Summerville had put the Dutch ahead twice, only for Japan to peg them back each time and take a point from the Group F opener.

What a start by Sweden! Yasin Ayari unleashes a thunderbolt from distance after the ball drops kindly into his path, handing the Scandinavians a deserved lead. One of the goals of the tournament so far!
Sweden keep their momentum after the hydration break, doubling their lead in fine style as Alexander Isak attacks down the left, cuts inside and steers the ball into the corner. What a start!

And just like that, Tunisia are back in it. Omar Rekik glances a beautiful header into the net just before the break — this could be a real momentum shift. It was Rekik's first international goal, and what a moment to score it!
It had been coming as Sweden cranked up the pressure early in the second half. This time Viktor Gyokeres is the scorer, after Isak pounces on a defensive error, making it 3-1 to Sweden.
This game has been bookended by goals from Yasin Ayari, who caps an outstanding Sweden display with a thunderbolt from distance.
Brian Brobbey, drafted in as a focal point for the Netherlands attack, converts Cody Gakpo's cross to finish off a sweeping Dutch move from close range.
A low, angled cross from the right by Denzel Dumfries is turned in by Brian Brobbey at full stretch for his third international goal.
Cody Gakpo strikes to finish a rapid Netherlands counter. The No11 cuts inside and fires unstoppably into the near post after being fed by impact sub Crysencio Summerville.
Cody Gakpo strikes to finish a rapid Netherlands counter. The No11 cuts inside and fires unstoppably into the near post after being fed by impact sub Crysencio Summerville.
Substitute Anthony Elanga scores for Sweden, staying calm to slot past the keeper after running onto Alexander Isak's superb through ball.

Netherlands 5-1 Sweden: What an impact from Crysencio Summerville, who both starts and finishes the move, driving to the edge of the box before unleashing an unerring low strike past the dive of Kristoffer Nordfeldt.

Daichi Kamada's fourth-minute goal is the fastest by a Japanese player in FIFA World Cup history. The previous quickest was Shinji Kagawa's sixth-minute strike against Colombia in 2018. Kamada is also just the second Japanese player to score in back-to-back World Cup matches, after Junichi Inamoto found the net against Belgium and Russia in 2002.
Japan are brilliant; Tunisia are on the brink. The Asian side double their lead in spectacular fashion, Ayase Ueda drilling an amazing strike into the bottom corner.

Japan stretch their lead again as Junya Ito bursts clear of the Tunisia defence and slots home in style. Tunisia are in the deepest of holes now. It's only the second time in 27 World Cup matches that Japan have scored three in a game — the last coming in 2010, when they beat Denmark 3-1 in South Africa.

What a display from Japan, who have scored four in a World Cup match for the first time. It comes from the inspirational Ayase Ueda, who hangs in the air before looping a header over the Tunisia defence and in. Ueda also becomes the first Japan player to score twice in a single World Cup match.

Both sides will feel they could have won it, but a draw suits each and keeps their North American adventures alive. Daizen Maeda broke the deadlock in the second half, applying the finish to a flowing Japan move 11 minutes after the restart. Sweden responded, though, striking back just six minutes later: Anthony Elanga collected the ball on the right, cut inside and curled a sumptuous left-footed effort into the corner. Graham Potter's side went closest to winning it when Alexander Isak's late header was tipped onto the bar by Zion Suzuki. Japan now meet Brazil in Houston on 29 June, while Sweden await their opponents.
Anthony Elanga scores in a second successive match! The winger collects it on the right, cuts inside and unleashes an unstoppable effort that flies past Suzuki. Game on!
The Dutch striker doubles the Oranje's lead and takes his personal tally at the tournament to three. Tunisia's defence was again unpicked by a crossfield ball and then a pass across the box.
Hazem Mastouri heads home from a corner. The game should open up more now, with the Eagles of Carthage looking sharper after the break.
His first goal for the national team gives the Dutch some breathing space as they battle for top spot in Group F.
What a way to open your international account! Eman Ashour drifts in from the left and delivers an exquisite finish with pace and swerve — the 28-year-old midfielder's first strike for his country.
Against the run of play, New Zealand find a way through Elijah Just, who lashes a volley into the net. Game on!

IR Iran have grown since the hydration break and draw level after an attacking effort from Shahriyar Moghanloo drops kindly for Ramin Rezaeian, who fires home with the outside of his boot, to the delight of the Team Melli fans at Los Angeles Stadium.

Elijah Just is the man of the moment, finding the target in style to put New Zealand ahead once more. A brilliant team move from the All Whites, which he finished superbly. Can New Zealand hold on for their first-ever World Cup win?
IR Iran have drawn level for a second time here, this through a towering Mohammad Mohebbi header. What a finish we have in store!
Finn Surman with the goal! A wonderful header from the defender, who climbs high to power home Tim Payne's corner. What a start for the All Whites!
It had been coming! Egypt's pressure tells. Mohamed Hany is the provider, swinging in an inviting cross that Zico heads goalward — Max Crocombe gets a hand to it, but the power is too much. 1-1!
He's done it! Egypt's captain has put his side ahead — and in style. Salah exchanges a delightful one-two with Zico before powering a precise drive past Crocombe. BC Place has erupted.

And breathe. Egypt and IR Iran have served up a show so far in Seattle. Saber opened the scoring in only the fifth minute, and it looked set to be cancelled out moments later when Taremi lined up a penalty — Shoebir denied him, but was powerless 135 seconds on to stop Rezaeian stealing in to level. Which side will seize the initiative after the hydration break?
IR Iran are level! What a game! Ramin Rezaeian is the man. Milad Mohammadi fires goalward and, though Shobeir saves, Rezaeian is on hand to turn it home from the tightest of angles. Game on!
It had been coming all game. Belgium go ahead as Leandro Trossard converts a Kevin De Bruyne corner, the ball dropping kindly into his path. As things stand, New Zealand are heading out.
Belgium begin the second half brightly, Trossard finding the net at the second attempt. New Zealand are in a deep hole now and staring at elimination.

Belgium's third all but seals their Round of 32 spot and eliminates New Zealand. Kevin De Bruyne, who'd spurned a few chances tonight, finally makes one count, guiding the ball into the corner. What a moment for the veteran.
Having scored twice against IR Iran, Elijah Just grabs his third of the tournament with another fantastic strike, this time against Belgium. The future looks bright for him.
Barely a minute after coming on, Romelu Lukaku finds the net with a powerful downward header to restore Belgium's three-goal cushion. He really gives the Red Devils a different attacking dimension.

After a sluggish start, Belgium end the group stage with an emphatic win over New Zealand. Alexis Saelemaekers makes it five with a brilliant strike following clever Lukaku hold-up play. The Red Devils march on to the knockouts, while New Zealand are out — a tough way for the All Whites to bow out.

La Celeste have their goal! A deserved one for Bielsa's men, who've pinned Saudi Arabia back in their own half throughout the second period. Al Owais did well with an initial save, but Maxi Araujo — denied a terrific early chance — makes no mistake this time.

One of the standout stats from Spain's opener was that central attacker Mikel Oyarzabal didn't manage a single touch in the first half-hour. Today has already been a far better day for him: look at that fantastic cross that Yamal turned in for the goal.
Spain have flown from the first minute, and those two Mikel Oyarzabal goals — his 26th and 27th for his country — have put them in complete control.
Spain have flown from the first minute, and those two Mikel Oyarzabal goals — his 26th and 27th for his country — have put them in complete control.
Can you believe it! Kevin Pina scores a historic goal with Cabo Verde's first chance of the game — and against the run of play.
La Celeste were pouring forward in waves, and Maxi Araujo finally breached the staunch Blue Sharks defence, heading home after Bentancur struck the post.
La Celeste stun Cabo Verde to lead 2-1 at the interval. Canobbio tapped home after a beautiful headed assist from Araujo.
Helio Varela came on just three minutes ago and, just like that, he's levelled it! Can Cabo Verde go one better?

Baena collects a cross from the right, finds space in front of two defenders and swings in a shot Muslera can't hold, the ball trickling over the line. Both Ugarte and Yamal were down injured as the move unfolded, but Baena's focus never wavered. Spain lead 1-0.

Kylian Mbappe has matched Lionel Messi's haul of 13 World Cup goals, reaching it in his 15th appearance to the Argentine's 26 games. Both Mbappe and Messi now sit three goals shy of Miroslav Klose's competition record.
Coming on for his PSG team-mate Ousmane Dembele, Bradley Barcala became the third young forward to make his World Cup debut for Les Bleus today ... and what a way to mark it, with a goal.

A word, too, for Senegal, who played well in the first half and came so close to opening the scoring through Nicolas Jackson's shot off the post and Ismaila Sarr's close-range effort over the bar. They carry plenty of attacking talent of their own, as shown by the goal from 18-year-old World Cup debutant Ibrahiim Mbaye.
Moments after that Senegal goal, Kylian Mbappe lashes in a long-range strike to move past Olivier Giroud as France's all-time record scorer.

A highly entertaining opening period, and Norway lead thanks to a double from you-know-who! A typically predatory Erling Haaland finish put them ahead, but Iraq responded impressively to level through a superb Aymen Hussein header. Haaland soon punished Iraq again, pouncing on a loose back pass towards goalkeeper Jalal Hassan to restore Norway's lead. Iraq finished the half strongly, though, piling on sustained pressure in search of another equaliser. Roll on the second half!

A highly entertaining opening period, and Norway lead thanks to a double from you-know-who! A typically predatory Erling Haaland finish put them ahead, but Iraq responded impressively to level through a superb Aymen Hussein header. Haaland soon punished Iraq again, pouncing on a loose back pass towards goalkeeper Jalal Hassan to restore Norway's lead. Iraq finished the half strongly, though, piling on sustained pressure in search of another equaliser. Roll on the second half!

A highly entertaining opening period, and Norway lead thanks to a double from you-know-who! A typically predatory Erling Haaland finish put them ahead, but Iraq responded impressively to level through a superb Aymen Hussein header. Haaland soon punished Iraq again, pouncing on a loose back pass towards goalkeeper Jalal Hassan to restore Norway's lead. Iraq finished the half strongly, though, piling on sustained pressure in search of another equaliser. Roll on the second half!

A stunner from Mbappe puts France ahead just shy of the quarter-hour. A quick give-and-go with Michael Olise, and the France captain fires his nation in front — with his father watching on from the stands.

A horrible mix-up at the back for Iraq, and Mbappe pounces to make it two. Zaid Tahseen's pass, meant for his keeper, ran straight to Dembele inside the box, and he rolled it back for Mbappe to sweep home the easiest of his now 16 World Cup goals.

France in complete control now as Ousmane Dembele makes it three. Michael Olise was the architect, gliding past several Iraq defenders and splitting several more with a pass to Dembele, whose first-time shot flashed past Ahmed Basil.
Marcus Holmgren Pedersen started on the bench, but once handed his chance the right-back took it in style, firing Norway ahead with his first international goal.

Talk about incisive! Martin Odegaard drives a 4-on-2 break before threading a perfectly weighted pass to the feet of Erling Haaland. He has team-mates to his right but doesn't need them, lifting a superb finish high past Edouard Mendy.

Sadio Mane does brilliantly to keep possession and the move alive down the left. Getting it back, he slides it to Ismaila Sarr, who shows tremendous strength before burying his finish past the onrushing Orjan Nyland. The Norway keeper gets a hand to it but can't keep it out.

Another World Cup double for one of the finest strikers around. Senegal fail to clear their lines from a ball in off the right, letting Antonio Nusa pounce and feed Patrick Berg to his left. The cross is spot on, and Haaland's finish — a volley off the underside of the bar — is even better.

Senegal pull one back through Ismaila Sarr's second of the game. A great first touch on the penalty spot takes him clear of his marker, and he makes no mistake with the finish. Around six minutes for the Lions of Teranga to find a leveller...

It takes six minutes for Ousmane Dembele to break the deadlock for France. Freed by Kylian Mbappe's crossfield pass, Dembele has acres of room to attack down the right before cutting inside to flash an unstoppable shot past Egil Selvik. His ninth international goal.
Ousmane Dembele again! The France forward takes a pass from Kylian Mbappe and cuts inside from the right as Mbappe makes the decoy run, letting Dembele curl a perfect finish away from the goalkeeper.

Norway pull one back! Winger Thelo Aasgaard — Norwegian father, French mother — dummies inside Dayot Upamecano and wrong-foots goalkeeper Mike Maignan with a fine low strike. No Les Bleus player touched the ball between France's second goal and Norway's instant reply.

Ousmane Dembele repeats his previous finish exactly to complete a 32nd-minute hat-trick. With the goalkeeper unsighted, Dembele flashes another unerring effort into the far post. It's the third treble of this tournament.
4-1: A beautiful header from Desire Doue, who redirects Bradley Barcola's cross past Egil Selvik. A goal that adds gloss to this French win.
Habib Diarra strikes first for Senegal, turning home Seck's header to put the Lions of Teranga ahead. A dream start for the African side.
From close range, Ismaila Sarr nets his fourth World Cup goal for Senegal, moving past Papa Bouba Diop as his country's all-time leading scorer at the tournament.
Just seconds after coming on as a substitute, Gueye unleashes a thunderous left-foot strike into the roof of the net from 20 yards. Unbelievable hit — one of the goals of the tournament so far.
He's done it again! Senegal make it four, and super-sub Gueye has his second. That one may have been nearer 100 km/h — simply impossible to save strikes like that.
Another wonder-goal for Senegal, this time from Ndiaye. Right foot. Fiercely hit. Top corner.
A GOLAZO from Messi! The legendary No10 picks the ball up centrally, surges towards the box and unleashes a left-footed laser past Zidane for his 14th... yes, 14th World Cup goal!!
Argentina double their lead as Zidane can't hold a sizzling Alexis Mac Allister strike from distance, and Messi is there to tidy up the rebound, coolly sweeping home. 15 World Cup goals!

He's done it again! Incredible! Messi benefits from a brilliant build-up and finishes as only he can, a superb left-footed strike completing his treble on the night. That's 16 World Cup goals, drawing him level with Miroslav Klose's all-time mark — and it's Messi's first World Cup hat-trick.
A wonderful curling effort from the top of the D by Romano Schmid fires Austria ahead — the forward's fourth international goal.
Another fantastic goal as Jordan level. A wonderful run and finish from the top scorer at last year's FIFA Arab Cup, his curling effort clipping the post on its way past Alexander Schlager.
A late penalty from Austria's record scorer takes his personal tally to 48 and secures the points for the Europeans.
Lionel Messi sweeps home his fourth goal of this tournament ... and his record 17th on the World Cup stage!

Lionel Messi, two days short of his 39th birthday, picks his moments — and what moments from world football's man of the hour. He knows exactly where to be to conjure his enduring magic. An incredible display from the Argentina skipper to secure his nation's second win of the campaign ... and break that World Cup scoring record.
Algeria have dominated the opening period, but Jordan soaked up the pressure and struck first as Nizar Alrashdan hits it beautifully with the outside of his boot.
It had been coming. Algeria draw level as Nadhir Benbouali climbs high to head in a corner — their first goal at a World Cup finals since Brazil 2014. What a finish we have in store?
The Austrian shrugged off some pain in his leg to chase down a long ball over the top and open the scoring.
Les Fennecs had looked dangerous, creating chance after chance. Belghali skilfully beat three defenders and smashed it into the roof of the net!
Algeria level through Riyad Mahrez! The former Manchester City man finishes sweetly at the near post. There's no way this ends 2-2.
This last-minute goal surely means Algeria will finish second! A smooth finish into the far corner with his weaker foot.
We all thought Mahrez had won it, but Sasa Kalajdzic has just headed Austria straight back into second place!
A stunning free-kick from Giovani Lo Celso. One of Argentina's nine changes draws a smile of approval from Lionel Messi, curling a peach of a left-footed free-kick home from the top of the box.
Mousa Al Tamari pulls one back for Jordan. A wonderful curling delivery from the right by Ehsan Haddad is met by the half-time substitute, who steers it past Emi Martinez to hand Jordan a lifeline.
Lionel Messi with a wonderful free-kick to extend both Argentina's lead and his own scoring record. The iconic No10 keeps it low, bending it around the wall and into the far corner.
Joao Neves nets his fourth goal for his country with a terrific header from Pedro Neto's left-wing cross.
A goal 52 years in the making as Yoane Wissa rises to steer a firm header beyond the reach of goalkeeper Diogo Costa from Arthur Masuaka's inswinging cross.
Colombia break the deadlock through Daniel Munoz. The right-back popped up in the box to flick in a beautiful Luis Diaz ball, extending Colombia's run of never playing out a goalless World Cup match.

Uzbekistan score their first-ever World Cup goal. The Asian side had been growing into the game over the past few minutes, and Shomurodov's powerful volley opened up enough for Fayzullaev to bundle in the rebound.
The Bayern winger was pivotal, delivering crosses and creating danger down the flank before netting Colombia's second.
With time almost up, La Tricolor add another to make sure they top the group by a two-goal margin! Campaz with the powerful header!
Ronaldo becomes the first man to score at six World Cups, sweeping home Cancelo's right-wing cross. Incredible!
Two for Ronaldo. Cool as you like with the finish — a superb Bruno Fernandes through-ball, and Ronaldo makes no mistake, sliding it into the bottom corner. Portugal are cruising now.
As we were saying... super-sub Rafael Leao makes it five for Portugal with a very tidy finish indeed.

It's taken 75 minutes, but Colombia have finally broken the deadlock through dangerman Daniel Munoz, his curling near-post effort going in off a deflection. Redemption for Munoz, who spurned a chance early on. The South Americans are on course for the knockouts.
Uzbekistan's bright start pays off as Eldor Shomurodov races onto a flicked header before lifting an audacious looped finish over Lionel Mpasi. Be sure to catch that one on the highlights!

The referee points straight to the spot after Abdukodir Khusanov catches the outstretched boot of Yoane Wissa, who picks himself up to send the keeper the wrong way. Congo DR are now just one goal from a Round of 32 place...

Congo DR score the goal that could send them through. Two substitutes combine as Fiston Mayele reacts quickest to Meschack Elia's deflected shot, dinking a delightful finish over the advancing Abduvohid Nematov.

Yoane Wissa with a great turn and 18-yard finish to seal it. Meschak Elia did superbly to keep the ball alive after Fiston Mayele dragged a shot wide. The angles were all against Wissa, but he made a chance and somehow found the far corner.
Noni Madueke was something of a surprise starter, but early on he's impressed — winning the penalty that led to Kane's conversion and making several incisive runs down the left.

The stars are shining at this World Cup, and Harry Kane has joined the party. A day after France's Kylian Mbappe and Norway's Erling Haaland bagged braces, plus Lionel Messi's sublime hat-trick, Kane is the latest goalscoring hero with a first-half double against Croatia. An epic Golden Boot race is brewing!

A wonderfully open first 45 minutes produced four goals, including a brace for England superstar Harry Kane and a dramatic strike in the final seconds before the break from Petar Musa, who plays his club football in Dallas. What else can happen here today???

Ghana have surely won it! Caleb Yirenkyi has scored to send the Ghana fans wild — and the Panama supporters into despair. It stems from a lightning counter, Thomas-Asante charging forward before slipping a precise pass into the box for Yirenkyi, left with the simplest of tasks to tap home.

Croatia break the deadlock! A deep cross to the back post finds Budimir, on as a second-half sub, all alone. A cruel blow for Panama, who'd been very good until now, but one lapse and Croatia's experience has made them pay.
A set-piece goal for Jude Bellingham, who stretches out a leg to poke Bukayo Saka's corner into the bottom corner under pressure from Jorge Gutierrez.
Harry Kane becomes England's all-time top scorer at World Cup finals on 11 goals, moving ahead of Gary Lineker, heading in Jude Bellingham's perfect cross after a positive run by the midfielder.
What a strike from the Inter Milan midfielder. Perfectly placed into the far corner.
After a VAR review, Luckassen's finish stands! The Black Stars have turned the game around, the goal lifting them into second place in the group.
Only 10 minutes between the two second-half goals. What a header from Vlasic! Croatia leap back into second place in the group. We have a thrilling finale on our hands.
